dialogueLines.text: Reed, I understand that you're pissed, but I need you to listen to me right now.

choices.text: Derry. Stop trying to run away and make excuses. Tell me what really happened.
Pissed doesn't even begin to cover it Derry. Cut the crap.'

dialogueLines.text: I'm being honest with you, but you're not letting me talk.

choices.text: I'm really trying to hear you out. How much more do you need to say?
It's hard to listen when you keep trying to come up with something. Just admit it, you broke the TV I bought and stole my car!

dialogueLines.text: Can you just give me a chance to talk?!

choices.text: I'm really trying to hear you out. How much more do you need to say?
It's hard to listen when you keep trying to come up with something. Just admit it, you broke the TV I bought and stole my car!

dialogueLines.text: You've never gotten this mad about anything before, Reed! Can we even talk at this point?

choices.text: Are you trying to say that I really shouldn't be as upset as I am?
Can you even take responsibility at this point?!

dialogueLines.text: Your car isn't even stolen, it's right there!

choices.text: Sure, but I woke up to a broken TV and my car missing, and no word from you. I'm allowed to be upset.
Just because you brought it back doesn't mean you didn't steal it. You'll say or do anything except take responsibility!

dialogueLines.text: Of course, you're allowed to be upset. I broke the TV by accident, and wanted to make it up to you, but I think you're going too far now.

choices.text: Yeah, maybe I grilled you a bit too harshly...
I'm going too far?! You broke the TV and took my car without telling me anything, but I'm the one going too far?!

dialogueLines.text: Take responsibility? Okay sure, I could own up to it more, but I still can't understand why you're so upset. This whole thing was blown way out of proportion.

choices.text: Maybe I am a little over the top now...
I don't think I've chewed you out enough yet, to be honest.

dialogueLines.text: Honestly, I could have been a lot better myself. I wanted to sweep the broken TV under the rug, so I went behind your back and took your car, but I promise I only had good intentions.

choices.text: Okay. I believe you, and I'm sorry for overreacting.
You always do dumb stuff like this Derry! You can't ever be straightforward. I don't think I can forgive you this time.

dialogueLines.text: Reed. You're actually freaking me out now. Are you hearing yourself? Are you really willing to blow things up between us because of this?

choices.text: Okay. I believe you, and I'm sorry for overreacting.
You always do dumb stuff like this Derry! You can't ever be straightforward. I don't think I can forgive you this time.

dialogueLines.text: Then I guess this can't be fixed. Not right now, anyways.
